name: Salesforce, Inc.

profile.description: Salesforce, Inc. is the world's largest CRM software company, generating $41.5B in FY2026 revenue across Sales Cloud, Service Cloud, Marketing Cloud, Slack, and its Agentforce AI platform. Under founder-CEO Marc Benioff, Salesforce has crossed an AI inflection point with Agentforce autonomous agents and Data Cloud, generating ~$1.4B ARR in combined AI and data products. Market cap ~$137B.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (3/3): Salesforce built the SaaS enterprise software category in 1999, pioneered cloud CRM, executed transformative acquisitions (Slack $27.7B, MuleSoft, Tableau), and launched Agentforce — a consistent 27-year record of category-defining moves representing multiple successful business model transformations [1][2]. AI Deployment (2/2): Agentforce is the most compelling AI product launch in enterprise software — Agentforce and Data 360 reached ~$1.4B ARR (up 114% YoY) with 18,500+ deals closed, and AI agents resolve 85% of Salesforce's own customer service inquiries with 84% self-resolution rates [1][2]. Market Share (2/2): Salesforce holds ~25% global CRM market share, sustained category leadership for two decades, with FY2026 subscription revenue growing 13% YoY to $10.7B in Q4 [1]. Product Pipeline (1/2): Agentforce monetization expanding through Agentic Work Units pricing, $60B FY2030 revenue target, but only 8% of customer base (~12,500 of ~150,000) has adopted Agentforce so far [3]. R&D Intensity (0/1): R&D spending is substantial in absolute terms but the company has frozen engineering hiring in favor of AI agents, making R&D intensity direction ambiguous.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): Salesforce's 1-1-1 philanthropic model (1% equity, 1% product, 1% employee time donated) and 'Ohana' culture represent one of the most distinctive corporate missions in tech, generating $700M+ in grants and 8M+ volunteer hours through Salesforce.org [2][5]. The mission is specific, thoughtful, and has shaped strategic decisions for 27 years. However, recent culture fractures from layoffs and political tensions prevent the highest score. Community Impact (2/2): Salesforce.org has distributed over $700M in grants, and the 1-1-1 model has been adopted by 18,000+ companies globally — a genuinely distinctive community investment with measurable impact [2]. Employee Retention (0/2): An estimated 13,000-14,000 employees have been laid off over 5 years, including ~4,000 support roles replaced by AI agents in 2025 with Benioff stating he 'needed less heads,' and ~1,000 additional cuts in early 2026 [5]. Top talent departures including the Co-CEO and head of Agentforce signal retention challenges. Insider Buying (1/2): Benioff holds ~3% insider ownership and has maintained long-term holdings, but no significant recent net insider buying was identified.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics — scores nearly perfectly across all dimensions. FY2026 revenue of $41.5B (10% YoY growth), GAAP operating margin of 20.1%, non-GAAP operating margin of 34.1%, free cash flow of $14.4B (up 16% YoY), and operating cash flow of $15.0B (up 15%) [1]. D/E of 29.9% scores perfectly (1.0). ROE of 12.4% scores 0.83 — slightly below 15% target but improving. Revenue growth of 12.1% YoY exceeds target. Forward P/E of 11.2 is remarkably low for a $41.5B revenue software company. PEG of 0.91 scores perfectly, signaling undervaluation relative to growth. Profit margin of 18.0% scores 0.90, approaching 20% target. 5yr revenue CAGR of 9.8% is near the 10% target. RPO of $72.4B provides exceptional forward visibility.

ai_resilience.label: MODERATE
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (6/9): Salesforce's enterprise CRM platform benefits from deep customer data moats, high switching costs, and 27 years of customer relationship data that create structural barriers to displacement [1]. However, Salesforce faces meaningful AI disruption risk from multiple angles: Microsoft Copilot Studio integrated with Dynamics 365 and M365 offers comparable AI agent capabilities with superior distribution; AI-native CRM startups could bypass traditional CRM entirely; and the shift to agentic AI could compress traditional per-seat SaaS pricing models if enterprises resist per-interaction Agentforce pricing [3]. The company is both beneficiary and potential victim of the AI revolution — existing business faces pricing pressure even as Agentforce grows. AI Opportunity (1/1): Agentforce and Data 360 generated ~$1.4B ARR (up 114% YoY) with 18,500+ deals, representing quantifiable AI-derived revenue that is the company's primary growth engine [1][2].

infinite_game.summary: Marc Benioff founded Salesforce 27 years ago with a mission to democratize enterprise software and has consistently pursued long-term transformation through cloud, mobile, AI, and now autonomous agents. The 1-1-1 philanthropic model, adopted before profitability, is genuine evidence of cause over optimization. However, the massive layoffs of 13,000-14,000 employees over 5 years — particularly replacing 4,000 support roles with AI while citing 'needing less heads' — creates a profound tension between the Ohana culture and actual employee treatment that Sinek would view critically.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): Salesforce's cause of democratizing enterprise software and the 1-1-1 model have been validated over 27 years of execution, with the philanthropic commitment predating profitability [2]. The $60B FY2030 vision and Agentforce represent genuine belief in transforming how enterprises work. However, the tension between 'Ohana' culture and laying off 13,000+ employees — with Benioff explicitly stating AI lets him need 'less heads' — undermines the inclusive, people-first elements of the Just Cause [5]. A genuine Just Cause cannot treat people as replaceable inputs.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (1/2): Benioff paid $27.7B for Slack when critics called it overvalued, invested a decade in Einstein AI before Agentforce became credible, and maintained the 1-1-1 model through downturns [2]. The decision to freeze engineering hiring in favor of AI coding agents is bold but controversial. However, the massive layoffs look more like cost optimization under activist pressure than courageous long-term investment — 7,000+ cuts in 2023 followed Elliott Management's involvement.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (0/2): The cumulative impact of ~13,000-14,000 layoffs over 5 years, including 4,000 support roles explicitly replaced by AI agents, has fundamentally damaged the Ohana trust culture [5]. Top talent departures including the Co-CEO and head of Agentforce signal organizational instability at the leadership level. Benioff's statement about needing 'less heads' because of AI directly contradicts the psychological safety that Sinek's Trusting Teams requires. Employees cannot feel safe when the CEO publicly celebrates headcount reduction.

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (2/2): Salesforce has fundamentally reinvented itself multiple times — from SaaS CRM to cloud platform (Force.com) to enterprise acquisition strategy (Tableau, MuleSoft, Slack) to AI agent platform (Agentforce) [1][2]. Each transformation was driven by the cause of empowering enterprises, not desperation. The operational decision to stop hiring engineers and rely on AI coding agents is among the most dramatic pivots any large enterprise software company has attempted.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): Benioff consistently frames Microsoft Dynamics/Copilot, ServiceNow, and HubSpot as competitive threats that push Salesforce to innovate [3]. Agentforce was explicitly designed to leapfrog Microsoft Copilot in enterprise AI agents. The competitive language focuses on building better products rather than destroying competitors.

investment_thesis: Salesforce delivered a strong FY2026 with $41.5B revenue (10% YoY growth), 34.1% non-GAAP operating margins, and $14.4B in free cash flow (up 16%) [1]. Agentforce and Data 360 reached ~$1.4B ARR (114% growth), with 18,500+ deals demonstrating enterprise adoption momentum. At 11.2x forward earnings and 0.91 PEG, Salesforce is remarkably cheap for a $41.5B revenue software company with 34% operating margins, $72.4B in RPO, and a credible AI growth narrative. The $60B FY2030 revenue target implies sustained low-teens growth augmented by Agentforce pricing upside. Q4 subscription revenue accelerated to 13% YoY growth, the strongest quarter of FY2026.
thesis_continued: The bear case centers on whether Agentforce can sustain its growth trajectory at scale. Only ~8% of Salesforce's ~150,000 customer base (~12,500) has adopted Agentforce, with only 6,000 as paid engagements — raising questions about conversion rates and willingness to pay for agentic AI [3]. Microsoft Copilot Studio with Dynamics 365 integration represents a credible competitive threat with superior distribution through M365. The AI transition creates structural uncertainty: per-agent pricing could cannibalize traditional per-seat revenue, and the shift to AI coding agents internally carries execution risk. Culturally, the ~13,000-14,000 cumulative layoffs including 4,000 AI-replaced support roles have damaged the Ohana brand, and top talent departures including the Co-CEO signal organizational instability [5]. Benioff at 60 creates succession uncertainty for a company deeply tied to its founder.
key_catalysts: Agentforce adoption accelerating beyond 8% of customer base — conversion of the remaining 137,000+ customers represents a massive TAM for per-agent and per-interaction pricing that could drive AI revenue from $1.4B to multi-billion ARR
FY2027 revenue guidance execution at ~$47B would confirm sustained low-teens organic growth plus Informatica contribution, validating the $60B FY2030 trajectory

Data Cloud becoming the enterprise AI data platform standard with 136% NDR among large customers, compounding the Agentforce moat by ensuring customer data flows through Salesforce's AI infrastructure
key_risks: Microsoft Copilot Studio competitive acceleration — Microsoft's M365 distribution advantage, Azure AI capabilities, and Dynamics 365 integration give it a structural path to offer comparable AI agents to the same enterprise customers Salesforce targets

Agentforce monetization falling short if enterprises resist per-agent or per-interaction pricing and revert to traditional seat-based economics — with only 6,000 paid engagements out of 12,500 adopters, conversion rates are unproven at scale

Marc Benioff succession uncertainty — Salesforce's product vision, partner relationships, and customer trust are deeply tied to Benioff personally; the Co-CEO departure and head of Agentforce exit signal leadership instability that could worsen
sources: Salesforce Q4 FY2026 Earnings Release — Record Revenue of $41.5B, investor.salesforce.com, February 25, 2026
Salesforce Q3 FY2026 Earnings Release — Agentforce and Data 360 Results, investor.salesforce.com, December 2025
Salesforce Agentforce Adoption and Marc Benioff AI Strategy — Fortune, Dreamforce Coverage, 2025-2026
Yahoo Finance — CRM Key Statistics and Financial Data, accessed May 2026
Salesforce Layoffs and ESG Controversy — CNBC, SalesforceBen, HRD America, 2023-2026
